_purpose: The live GraphQL contract the belt-gateway PROMISES its consumers. Producer-side guard: CI introspects the live schema and fails the moment a consumer-depended field drifts or disappears — catching the class of break where a consumer was built against an ASSUMED schema. Origin: the loa-freeside shadow-audit (#296) shipped a query selecting logIndex/txHash/value that don't exist on the live Transfer type, so it 400'd on the first page fetch and no audit could run (found + fixed 2026-06-23, loa-freeside#300). This guard makes that class impossible to re-introduce silently.

Transfer.consumer: loa-freeside/packages/adapters/sonar — Transfer-replay holder reconstruction (the Shadow-Mode audit's data layer)
invariants: logIndex is NOT a column — it is encoded in id as `<transactionHash>_<logIndex>`. Consumers derive it, never select it.
value is NOT served — Transfer is ERC-721-only; ERC-1155 amounts live in separate entities.
the tx hash field is `transactionHash`, NOT `txHash`.
